articles.title: COINDCX DISMISSES REPORT OF COINBASE ACQUISITION TALKS
articles.summary: CoinDCX's CEO Sumit Gupta flatly denied reports that Coinbase is in advanced talks to acquire the Indian exchange, reiterating that the company remains “super focused” on building for India's crypto market. A report from Mint had suggested Coinbase might value CoinDCX at under $1 billion, down sharply from its $2.2 billion 2021 valuation, and noted Coinbase already holds equity in CoinDCX and rival CoinSwitch. Coinbase declined to comment on the rumor, pointing only to its broader mission and ongoing global exploration of build, buy, partner, and invest opportunities.

articles.title: BITMINE APPROVES $1B STOCK REPURCHASE PROGRAM
articles.summary: BitMine, now focused on becoming a major Ethereum treasury, approved a $1 billion stock repurchase program while disclosing 625,000 ETH ($2.4B) and 192 BTC in reserves. Chair Tom Lee said buying back shares may offer a better return than ETH in pursuit of the firm's goal to stake 5% of the ETH supply. BitMine's NAV per share stands at $22.76, and it now ranks among the top publicly listed ETH-holding firms.

articles.title: KRAKEN TO INTEGRATE INK TOKEN AND INK NETWORK INTO 'CORE PRODUCT SUITE'
articles.summary: Kraken will integrate the INK token and Ink Layer 2 into its core product suite for all global clients, including via the Kraken Drops airdrop program. The Ink Foundation designed INK to drive network utility rather than governance control. By embedding Ink-native protocols, Kraken aims to bridge CeFi and DeFi, aligning onchain and off‑chain infrastructure and standardizing value movement across its trading and payment systems. Its first use case for INK will be an Aave liquidity pool on Ink that offers concentrated liquidity as a foundational DeFi building block.

articles.title: LINEA UNVEILS NATIVE ETH YIELD BEFORE TOKEN ROLLOUT
articles.summary: Linea, the Consensys-backed Ethereum L2, will introduce native ETH staking, a protocol-level ETH burn (20% of fees), and a deflationary LINEA token that burns 80% of fees. 85% of the token supply will go to the ecosystem, 10% for early users, and 75% via a 10-year Ethereum-aligned fund managed by a consortium including Consensys, Eigen Labs, ENS Labs, and SharpLink. The fund launches on Ethereum's 10th anniversary, aiming to make Linea a core institutional and community layer for ETH capital.

articles.title: HYPERLIQUID VALUATION FRAMEWORK
articles.summary: Using a conservative $3 million/day revenue base case and adjusting for recurring stock‑based compensation, Hyperliquid trades at attractive multiples versus public peers. A projected bull scenario of capturing 25% of the perps market could drive fees to $15 million/day, supported by Hyperliquid's superior free‑cash‑flow margins, lean 12‑person team, and $300 million in TradFi dry powder via SONN. Distribution remains a bottleneck with only 153k holders, but partnerships like Phantom Perps and builder codes, upcoming fiat on‑ramps, HIP‑3 sinks, spot‑collateral perps, and new listings promise to broaden access.

articles.title: COINBASE BETS ON STABLECOINS AND AI TO TRANSFORM E-COMMERCE
articles.summary: Coinbase VP Shan Aggarwal argues that stablecoins will serve as the native payment rails for autonomous AI agents, ushering in a new era of agentic commerce where bots and humans transact seamlessly. To capture this opportunity, Coinbase is building infrastructure like x402 and AgentKit to enable AI-driven wallets to hold, send, and receive stablecoins around the clock. By pairing stablecoin rails with self‑custodial wallets, the exchange aims to replace legacy credit and debit networks and unlock digital commerce for billions, especially in underserved markets. Through ecosystem integration and backing of crypto‑AI startups via Coinbase Ventures, the company is positioning itself as the default platform for the emerging machine‑economy.

articles.title: STABLE: A DIGITAL NATION OF USDT, BY USDT, FOR USDT
articles.summary: Viewing blockchains as “digital nations,” USDT is already a dominant reserve currency, yet it operates on chains not purposely built for it. Stable has proposed an L1 expressly optimized for USDT, with account‑abstraction allowing users to pay gas with USDT0, fee-free transfers, and enterprise features such as guaranteed blockspace, Transfer Aggregator, and confidential ZK payments for remittances, payments, and DeFi.

articles.title: STABLECOINS DO CREATE CREDIT, JUST NOT LIKE BANKS
articles.summary: Stablecoins, such as Circle's USDC, actively create credit through their reserves, including treasuries (loans to the government), repos (secured loans to financial institutions), and bank deposits (loans to banks). This structure qualifies as credit creation, even without fractional lending. Banks account for just 20% of U.S. credit, with trillions more generated through money market funds and securitized debt like MBS. Narrow-bank stablecoins are less prone to runs, need no bailouts, and offer safer intermediation, making them a financial upgrade, not a threat.

articles.title: BITMINE AUTHORIZES $1B BUYBACK DESPITE MNAV PREMIUM
articles.summary: BitMine, the largest Ethereum treasury company, announced a $1 billion share repurchase authorization, but the stock currently trades at a premium to mNAV.

articles.title: ONCHAIN MEDIA MONETIZATION: TWO MODELS AND A QUESTION
articles.summary: Scalable onchain media could take the path of a high-stakes investment game for users seeking outsized returns, or as a pro-tier investment feature used by a minority, where the majority benefits passively from the market's information outputs similar to Polymarket.

articles.title: RAKBANK LAUNCHES DIRECT CRYPTO TRADING IN AED
articles.summary: RAKBANK has become the first UAE bank to offer retail crypto trading, enabling users to buy and sell BTC, ETH, SOL, and XRP directly in dirhams (AED).

articles.title: $464M LIQUIDATED AS ETH, BTC LEAD MARKET REBOUND
